Title: De novo Biosynthesis of “Non-Natural” Thaxtomin Phytotoxins

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates and incorporates L-phenylalanine into the thaxtomin diketopiperazine assembly line together with TxtB during nonribosomal peptide formation
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Thaxtomin diketopiperazine intermediate with incorporated phenylalanine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly required for assembly of thaxtomin scaffold
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Thaxtomin D analogues; thaxtomin A analogues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxins that inhibit cellulose biosynthesis in plants; herbicidal activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture supernatant of engineered Streptomyces albus fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Required for heterologous production of thaxtomin D and downstream hydroxylated thaxtomin analogues in S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. txtA from S. scabies was amplified and assembled with txtB into a synthetic operon for heterologous expression in S. albus. 2. Expression of txtA with txtB enabled production of thaxtomin D after feeding 4-nitrotryptophan. 3. The txtAB strain accepted several 4-substituted tryptophan derivatives, yielding new thaxtomin D analogues.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed during fermentation in engineered S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with 4-nitrotryptophan or substituted tryptophan/indole precursors during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Substrate incorporation was limited for more polar analogues, constraining product diversity
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in S. albus; precursor feeding; RP-HPLC purification; LC-MS/MS product identification
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful as a core assembly enzyme for synthetic biology production of natural and non-natural thaxtomin herbicide analogues in tractable hosts
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: txtA was engineered with optimized ribosomal binding site and spacer sequence for synthetic operon construction

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Nonribosomal peptide synthetase (NRPS) module with amino acid adenylation specificity
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Midstream peptide assembly and substrate selection step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Activates and incorporates L-tryptophan or substituted tryptophan derivatives into the thaxtomin diketopiperazine scaffold together with TxtA, followed by condensation/N-methylation/cyclization in the NRPS assembly line
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Thaxtomin D and non-natural thaxtomin D analogues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Directly forms target thaxtomin scaffold from precursor amino acids
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: Thaxtomin D analogues; thaxtomin A analogues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxins that inhibit cellulose biosynthesis in plants; herbicidal activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture supernatant of engineered Streptomyces albus fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Determines incorporation of alternative tryptophan-derived substituents into thaxtomin analogues; accepted halogenated and methylated analogues but poorly accepted more polar substrates
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. txtB coexpressed with txtA in S. albus enabled production of thaxtomin D from fed 4-nitrotryptophan. 2. txtB-containing NRPS accepted several C4-substituted tryptophan analogues, producing new thaxtomin D analogues with Cl, Br, F, and Me groups. 3. Failure to incorporate polar analogues suggested substrate selectivity/A-domain specificity as a major determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed during fermentation in engineered S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with substituted tryptophans or indoles during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: A-domain specificity limited incorporation of polar substrates, restricting analogue scope
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Branch/selectivity control point for analogue incorporation; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous expression in S. albus; precursor feeding; comparative control strains; HPLC and LC-MS/MS metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: High value for pathway engineering of thaxtomin analogues; engineering A-domain specificity could expand non-natural herbicide diversity
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A new desnitro thaxtomin containing unmodified tryptophan was detected, implying TxtB can load L-tryptophan when 4-nitrotryptophan is not dominant

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Hydroxylates the phenylalanine residue of thaxtomin intermediates first at the alpha-position and then at aryl positions, generating singly and doubly hydroxylated thaxtomins including thaxtomin A
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Thaxtomin D and non-natural thaxtomin D analogues
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Thaxtomin A and hydroxylated thaxtomin analogues
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts thaxtomin scaffold to more active hydroxylated target products
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: More potent herbicidal phytotoxins, largely due to increased solubility, that inhibit cellulose biosynthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture supernatant of engineered Streptomyces albus fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Introduced hydroxyl groups into thaxtomin D and analogue scaffolds, producing thaxtomin A and new hydroxylated analogues with improved herbicidal relevance
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Addition of txtC to txtAB in S. albus enabled formation of thaxtomin A from fed 4-nitrotryptophan. 2. txtC also converted halogenated, methylated, and desnitro thaxtomin intermediates to hydroxylated analogues. 3. Product profiles indicated relaxed regioselectivity and support a sequential hydroxylation model.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed during fermentation in engineered S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with 4-nitrotryptophan or substituted tryptophans during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Regioisomer mixtures and incomplete conversion produced product heterogeneity
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Downstream activity-determining tailoring step; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Heterologous pathway reconstruction in S. albus; comparison with authentic thaxtomin A from S. scabies; HPLC retention time; MS/MS and accurate mass analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Important tailoring enzyme for generating more soluble and potent thaxtomin herbicide analogues; regioselectivity engineering may improve product definition
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper supports relaxed regioselectivity of TxtC and is consistent with initial alpha-hydroxylation before second aromatic hydroxylation

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply pathway for thaxtomin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Functions with TrpB in tryptophan synthase to generate indole-derived tryptophan analogues in vivo from fed indole derivatives and serine
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Substrate: Indole derivatives (with channeling to TrpB reaction context)
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: Substituted tryptophan intermediates for thaxtomin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for non-natural thaxtomin production
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxins that inhibit cellulose biosynthesis in plants; herbicidal activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture supernatant of engineered Streptomyces albus fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Enabled de novo production of thaxtomin analogues directly from simple indole precursors when coexpressed with trpB and txtAB in S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Codon-optimized trpA from S. typhimurium was combined with trpB and txtAB in S. albus. 2. The resulting strain produced thaxtomin D analogues directly from fed indole analogues. 3. Control strains lacking heterologous trpAB produced only trace analogues, confirming the engineered precursor-supply route.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed during fermentation in engineered S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with commercially available substituted indoles during fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Did not overcome failure of polar indole-derived analogues to enter the final product set, implying downstream specificity limits
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Upstream precursor supply step; key for de novo route but not established as rate-limiting
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Codon-optimized heterologous expression in S. albus; indole feeding; comparison to control strains lacking trpAB; LC-MS metabolite detection
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Valuable for constructing de novo biosynthetic routes from cheap indole feedstocks to non-natural thaxtomin herbicide analogues
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper primarily demonstrated TrpS function as trpAB pair rather than dissecting trpA independently

Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: Engineered precursor supply pathway for thaxtomin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes condensation of indole derivatives with L-serine to form substituted L-tryptophan analogues, functioning with TrpA in tryptophan synthase
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for downstream incorporation into thaxtomin analogues
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxins that inhibit cellulose biosynthesis in plants; herbicidal activity
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Culture supernatant of engineered Streptomyces albus fermentation
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promiscuous precursor-forming enzyme enabling de novo biosynthesis of non-natural thaxtomin analogues from substituted indoles
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TrpS from S. typhimurium was shown to accept diverse 4-substituted indoles and generate tryptophan analogues. 2. Codon-optimized trpB with trpA enabled in vivo conversion of indole analogues to precursors for thaxtomin biosynthesis in S. albus. 3. Presence of trpAB was necessary for robust analogue production from indoles.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Heterologously expressed during fermentation in engineered S. albus
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Feeding with 4-substituted indole analogues; PLP-dependent reaction context established in vitro
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Product scope still constrained by downstream NRPS substrate specificity for polar analogues
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Key upstream precursor-supply enzyme for de novo route; rate-limiting status not established
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: In vitro lysate assay with indole analogues; heterologous expression in S. albus; feeding experiments; control strains; LC-MS/HPLC product analysis
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong synthetic biology utility for generating diverse tryptophan-derived building blocks from inexpensive indoles in engineered production strains
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: The paper refers to the promiscuous tryptophan synthase as TrpS; mechanistically the beta subunit performs the PLP-dependent condensation step with TrpA support

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Produces nitric oxide used by TxtE for nitration of L-tryptophan at the 4-position
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies cosubstrate for formation of the natural nitrotryptophan precursor
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin inhibiting cellulose biosynthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Provides nitric oxide for natural 4-nitrotryptophan biosynthesis in the native pathway
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TxtD is described as the nitric oxide synthase in natural thaxtomin biosynthesis. 2. Its product NO is utilized by TxtE for 4-nitration of tryptophan. 3. It was background pathway context and not a direct experimental target in the engineered heterologous system.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Background knowledge cited in paper; not directly validated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Could be used with TxtE to reconstruct natural nitrotryptophan supply, but was not part of the core engineered route here
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included for pathway completeness, but not a core experimentally manipulated gene in this study

Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Uses nitric oxide and oxygen to nitrate L-tryptophan at the 4-position, forming L-4-nitrotryptophan
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Forms immediate precursor for natural thaxtomin biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Phytotoxin inhibiting cellulose biosynthesis in plants
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Generates the unusual nitrotryptophan precursor used in natural thaxtomin production
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. TxtE is described as the P450 enzyme catalyzing 4-nitration of L-tryptophan using NO and O2. 2. This step forms the unusual natural precursor L-4-nitrotryptophan. 3. TxtE was pathway background and not experimentally reconstructed in the heterologous system of this paper.
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Oxygen availability is required in reaction description
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Background knowledge cited in paper; not directly validated in this study
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for reconstructing natural nitrothaxtomin biosynthesis, though the current study bypassed it by precursor feeding or TrpS-based engineering
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included for pathway completeness, but not a core experimentally manipulated gene in this study
